task_objective: Keep launch-gated games paused through observed incoming Syncthing activity, recheck Ludusavi after settlement, preserve active transfer status precedence, and make conflict dismissal explicit.
tests_added: Pre-game activity records mutation, resets settlement on interleaving activity, and resolves only after three distinct settled samples.
Repeated pre-game download samples publish once while download-to-upload and idle-to-new-transfer transitions remain visible.
Idle, settled, unavailable, timeout, cancellation, supersession, late allocation, and post-game session misuse have typed quiescence outcomes.
The launch lease remains held through settlement and a fresh check; interrupted activity fails once without restore or conflict mutation and still resumes.
Pre-game local_current cannot replace a visible transfer, while only the post-game backed-up handoff receives the 900 ms dwell.
SAVE CONFLICT remains visible for the modal lifetime and conflict dismissal renders SYNC SKIPPED — CONFLICT UNRESOLVED in amber for the normal result duration.

red_tests.frontend_result: Expected RED: 21 failures named the missing quiescence API/effect, pre-game semantic deduplication, launch settlement gating, safe interrupted-transfer decision, lifecycle provenance dwell, persistent conflict, explicit conflict_unresolved rendering, and active-transfer precedence.
red_tests.diagram_result: Expected RED: the lifecycle diagram lacked the unresolved-conflict copy and settle/recheck sequence; the focused Python run also triggered the repository-wide coverage threshold.

design_decisions: Readiness and quiescence are separate generation-scoped promises; every terminal path settles both so callers cannot be stranded.
Pre-game waits return a discriminated result with activity provenance so unavailable-before-activity stays benign while failure-after-activity is unsafe.
Only an observed-and-settled watch discards the first check and reruns check_game_start, and both wait and recheck execute under PauseLeaseHandle.runProtected().
Status completion carries explicit lifecycle provenance; dwell eligibility is recorded only for lifecycle_exit backed_up completion.
Active conflict and dismissed conflict use separate status kinds because their visibility contracts differ.

results.implementation: Implemented typed pre-game settlement, bounded cancellation-safe waiting, protected post-settlement rechecks, status precedence, phase-specific dwell, and explicit conflict dismissal.
results.release: No release was dispatched during the implementation round; approved finalization owns merge, push, and development prerelease publication.
results.deferred_work: Steam Deck verification is deferred until the approved development prerelease is published, as required by the plan.

validation.result: Passed: 46 tests before the final controller regression additions; the complete focused/full result is recorded below.
Passed: 72 tests before the final controller regression additions; the complete focused/full result is recorded below.
Passed after correcting the regression-test state annotation.

All frontend gates and 686 Python tests passed; the run correctly failed because the three expanded TypeScript modules exceeded their existing line budgets. The implementation was consolidated without changing any budget.
Passed with gameLifecycleController.tsx at 450 lines, gameLifecycleDecision.ts at 255 lines, and syncthingMonitor.ts at 565 lines.
Passed: 4 documentation-contract tests. Coverage was disabled only for this focused run; the full gate below enforced repository coverage.
Passed: 283 Vitest tests, TypeScript typecheck, frontend build, Ruff check/format, ty, 687 pytest tests, 87.66% coverage, module budgets, and review-note deletion integrity.
